---
pagetitle: "utku turk"
page-layout: article
title-block-banner: false
comments: false
sidebar: false

execute:
  echo: false
  freeze: false


listing:
  contents: posts
  sort: "date desc"
  template: essay-listing.ejs
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  feed: true
---

<style>
.home-hero {
  margin: 1rem 0 2rem;
  padding-bottom: 0.5rem;
}

.home-hero-align {
  max-width: 1084px;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding-left: 2rem;
  padding-right: 1.1rem;
}

.home-hero-copy {
  width: 100%;
}

.home-hero-copy p {
  margin: 0 0 0.8rem;
  line-height: 1.45;
}

.home-hero-note {
  color: #555;
  font-size: 0.95em;
  font-style: italic;
}

.home-news {
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  line-height: 1.25;
}

.home-hero-sidebar-photo {
  text-align: center;
  margin-top: 0.2rem;
}

.home-hero-sidebar-photo img {
  width: 185px;
  max-width: 100%;
  height: auto;
  border-radius: 8px;
  display: block;
  margin: 0 auto;
}

.home-news h3 {
  margin-top: 0;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}

h2[data-anchor-id="essays"],
h3[data-anchor-id="news"] {
  margin-top: 0;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  text-transform: lowercase;
}

.home-news ul {
  padding-left: 0.9rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
}

.home-news li {
  margin: 0;
}

.home-news li + li {
  margin-top: 1.15rem;
}

.home-news time {
  font-weight: 600;
  font-size: 0.95em;
}

.home-news-more {
  display: block;
  text-align: right;
  margin-top: 0.25rem;
  font-size: 0.9em;
}

h2[data-anchor-id="essays"] .anchorjs-link,
h3[data-anchor-id="news"] .anchorjs-link {
  display: none !important;
}

@media (max-width: 991.98px) {
  .home-hero-align {
    padding-left: 0.75rem;
    padding-right: 0.75rem;
  }
}
</style>

::: {.home-hero .page-columns .page-full}
<div class="home-hero-align">
<div class="home-hero-copy">
<p>I’m a PhD candidate in Linguistics at the University of Maryland, working with <a href="https://www.ellenlau.net" target="_blank">Ellen Lau</a> and <a href="https://www.colinphillips.net" target="_blank">Colin Phillips</a>. My research investigates how different kinds of linguistic information—<strong>morphosyntactic structure</strong> and <strong>morphophonological form</strong>—are planned in production and retrieved during sentence processing, and how mismatches between them drive <strong>errors and create illusions</strong> in production and comprehension. I also work in formal properties of case and adjectival morphophonology, as well as question semantics and embedding facts.</p>
<p>I recently visited UMass to work with <a href="https://websites.umass.edu/snegishi/" target="_blank">Shota Momma</a> and <a href="https://farukakkus.wordpress.com" target="_blank">Faruk Akkus</a>. Previously, I received my MA from Boğaziçi University (advisor: <a href="https://github.com/plogacev" target="_blank">Pavel Logačev</a>) and was a visiting researcher at Masaryk University (advisor: <a href="https://www.muni.cz/en/people/53172-pavel-caha" target="_blank">Pavel Caha</a>).</p>
<p class="home-hero-note">My favorite food is <em>gata</em> with <em>koritz</em> and my favorite icecream flavor is saffron and rose (5pm snack of Anthony Bourdain). In my freetime, I like doing <a href="/calligraphy/">calligraphy</a>.</p>
</div>
</div>

::: {.column-margin .home-hero-sidebar-photo}
![](/images/utkuturkumd.jpeg)
:::
:::

## essays

::: {.column-margin .home-news}
### news
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
library(yaml)
HOWMANY = 10

`%||%` <- function(a, b) if (is.null(a) || identical(a, "")) b else a

parse_news_date <- function(s) {
  s <- trimws(as.character(s %||% ""))
  if (grepl("^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$", s)) return(as.Date(s))
  if (grepl("^\\d{4}-\\d{2}$", s)) return(as.Date(paste0(s, "-01")))
  if (grepl("^\\d{2}/\\d{4}$", s)) {
    p <- strsplit(s, "/", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    return(as.Date(sprintf("%s-%s-01", p[2], p[1])))
  }
  if (grepl("^\\d{4}$", s)) return(as.Date(paste0(s, "-01-01")))
  as.Date(NA)
}

fmt_news_date <- function(d) if (is.na(d)) "" else format(d, "%b %Y")

escape_html <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("&", "&amp;", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("<", "&lt;", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub(">", "&gt;", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x
}

valid_chr <- function(x) {
  !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0 && !is.na(x) && nzchar(as.character(x))
}

link_html <- function(url, label) {
  if (!valid_chr(url)) return(NULL)
  sprintf('<a href="%s">%s</a>', escape_html(as.character(url)), label)
}

render_home_news <- function(limit = 10) {
  paths <- c("_data/news.yaml", "data/news.yaml")
  path <- paths[which(file.exists(paths))[1]]
  if (is.na(path)) return('<ul><li><em>news.yaml not found</em></li></ul>')

  x <- yaml::read_yaml(path)
  items <- if (!is.null(x$news)) x$news else x

  rows <- lapply(items, function(it) {
    d <- parse_news_date(it$date %||% "")
    links <- c(
      link_html(it$hlink %||% NULL, "handout"),
      link_html(it$alink %||% NULL, "abstract"),
      link_html(it$slink %||% NULL, "slides"),
      link_html(it$pdf %||% NULL, "pdf")
    )
    if (valid_chr(it$extra %||% NULL)) links <- c(links, as.character(it$extra))

    list(
      date = d,
      when = fmt_news_date(d),
      desc = as.character(it$description %||% ""),
      links = links
    )
  })

  ord <- order(
    vapply(rows, function(r) if (is.na(r$date)) as.Date("0001-01-01") else r$date, as.Date("0001-01-01")),
    decreasing = TRUE
  )
  rows <- head(rows[ord], limit)

  item_html <- vapply(rows, function(r) {
    suffix <- if (length(r$links)) paste0(" · ", paste(r$links, collapse = " · ")) else ""
    sprintf("<li><time>%s</time> %s%s</li>", r$when, escape_html(r$desc), suffix)
  }, character(1))

  paste0("<ul>\n", paste(item_html, collapse = "\n"), "\n</ul>")
}

knitr::asis_output(render_home_news(limit = HOWMANY))
```

<br>
[More news →](/news/){.home-news-more}
:::
